5. Schema export does NOT modify metadata/ (META-006).
6. NFR-I3: schema is distributed with the CLI (embedded), not fetched
   from the network.

Do NOT:
- Hand-author the schema. Generate from Rust types via schemars (or an
  equivalent maintained crate; verify under cargo deny).
- Embed cross-reference autocomplete via $ref to remote URLs — schemas
  are local-first.

title: PostgreSQL DDL generator — full Phase 2A scope
description:
Promote the Phase 1 PoC PostgreSQL DDL generator to full Phase 2A scope:
all entities, FKs, unique constraints, NOT NULL, check constraints,
indexes where configured, comments, target version compatibility.
Implementation is split across four child tasks (63g.7.1 IR, 63g.7.2
emitter, 63g.7.3 safety gates, 63g.7.4 live-PG validation); this parent
bead is the integration story.

PRD refs: §11.2 (PostgreSQL DDL spec), §8.9 (DDL pipeline + safety
rules), GEN-001, NFR-I1 (compatibility matrix), §22 build sequence #13.

Output content (PRD §11.2):
- schemas (one per domain or per module per project config)
- tables for each entity
- columns mapped from logical type + semantic type via policy
- primary keys from business_key
- foreign keys from relationships
- unique constraints from attribute.unique = true
- not-null constraints from attribute.required = true
- check constraints from attribute.constraints (e.g., max_length for
  email semantic type)
- indexes where attribute or relationship metadata declares them
- COMMENT ON TABLE / COMMENT ON COLUMN where target supports

Compatibility:
- target_database.version controls dialect emitter behavior.
- compatibility_floor enforces the lowest version constructs (PRD §11.2:
  default greenfield floor PG 16).
- Version-incompatible constructs produce warnings or errors
  (META-005 acceptance #2).

Safety (NFR-S1, S2; AGENTS.md §4.4):
- Typed DDL IR (63g.7.1); no raw string interpolation for
  identifiers/literals.
- Identifiers validated against PG identifier rules + length limit
  (63 bytes by default) before IR construction (63g.7.3).
- Generated SQL parse-validated before write (63g.7.3 via
  sqlparser-rs).
- CI executes generated DDL against PG 16/17/18 in matrix (63g.7.4
  + NFR-R4, §20.1).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each child task's acceptance criteria pass (63g.7.1 IR, 63g.7.2
   deterministic emitter, 63g.7.3 safety gates, 63g.7.4 live-PG
   matrix).
2. Integration: the four child tasks compose into a single end-to-end
   pipeline (IR -> emitter -> identifier validation -> live PG
   matrix) that is green on Linux/macOS/Windows for the demo + 100-
   entity fixtures, *after* all four child tasks land. This is the
   parent-only integration assertion; subtasks own their per-layer
   tests.
3. Manifest records each artifact with metadata_object_ids and
   trace spans pointing at the lines covering each entity / attribute.
4. Performance: 10-entity full generation <5 s, 100-entity <60 s
   (NFR-P4, NFR-P5; absolute budgets — bsf.1 owns regression gate).

Do NOT:
- Embed timestamps or user names in SQL comments.
- Use `IF EXISTS` clauses that hide unintended drops; emit explicit DDL
  meant to be run by a migration tool.
- Generate any DROP statements in normal generation. DROPs are part of
  the migration-stub layer (LIFE-004, Phase 3 — hrw.4).
- Re-author subtask ACs here; this parent owns the integration story
  only.

title: Typed DDL IR — schemas, tables, columns, PK/FK/UQ/CHECK, indexes
description:
The know_now_ir crate carries the typed Postgres DDL IR. Other dialect
generators (Snowflake, BigQuery, DuckDB — Phase 4) will share this IR
or extend it.

PRD refs: §8.9, NFR-S1, NFR-SC2, AGENTS.md §4.4.

IR shape (sketch):
- LogicalSchema { schemas: Vec<SchemaDef> }
- SchemaDef { name: Identifier, tables: Vec<TableDef>, comment: Option<Doc> }
- TableDef { name, columns, primary_key, unique, foreign_keys, checks,
  indexes, comment }
- ColumnDef { name, ty: SqlType, not_null, default: Option<Literal>,
  comment }
- SqlType: typed (Varchar(n), Integer, Numeric(p,s), Timestamptz, Uuid,
  Json, Bytea, Boolean, ...)
- Identifier: newtype around String with validation invariants enforced
  by the constructor (no raw construction outside the crate).
- Literal: typed enum (Null | Int | Float | Bool | String | Bytea | ...);
  String literals serialize with proper quoting + escaping.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Identifiers cannot be constructed from arbitrary strings outside the
   IR crate (newtype + private field, factory functions enforce rules).
2. SqlType cardinality covers all Phase 2 logical types (PRD §10.7).
3. Round-trip test: lower an entity -> IR -> SQL -> reparse -> IR; the
   roundtrip is idempotent.
4. IR is Send + Sync + Clone for parallel emission (NFR-P13).

Do NOT:
- Expose String where an Identifier or Doc newtype would do
  (AGENTS.md §6.1).
- Allow dialect-specific syntax to leak into the IR (the IR is
  dialect-agnostic; the emitter resolves dialect choices).

title: PostgreSQL deterministic SQL emitter (PG 16/17/18)
description:
Emit deterministic SQL from the Postgres DDL IR. Three target versions
must be supported in Phase 2A: PG 16 (compatibility floor default), PG
17, PG 18 (default target for new demo projects per §11.2).

PRD refs: §8.9, §11.2, NFR-R1, NFR-PO3.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Output is sorted: schemas alphabetically, tables alphabetically
   within schema, columns in declared order from the entity.
2. Identifiers are quoted only when necessary (matches PG conventions);
   the emitter's quoting decision is deterministic across runs.
3. Numeric literals serialize with stable formatting (no locale).
4. Stable line endings: LF on every platform (NFR-PO3).
5. Trailing newlines: exactly one at end-of-file.
6. Comments use -- for single-line, /* */ for block where needed.
7. Emitter does NOT depend on any generator-specific state — pure
   function from IR to String.

Do NOT:
- Use system locale for any formatting decision.
- Emit ANSI escape codes or coloring.

title: Identifier validation + parser-validation gate
description:
Two safety nets for generated DDL:

1. Identifier validation BEFORE IR construction (NFR-S2, AGENTS.md §4.4):
   every metadata identifier mapped to an SQL identifier is validated
   for charset, length, and reserved-word collision.

2. Parser-validation gate AFTER emission (NFR-S3, GEN-008): the emitted
   SQL string is parsed via sqlparser-rs (or equivalent) before being
   passed to the writer. Failure aborts generation.

PRD refs: §8.9, §11.7, NFR-S1, NFR-S2, NFR-S3, AGENTS.md §4.4.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Invalid identifier rejection has a unit test per category:
   - too long (> 63 bytes for PG)
   - contains illegal chars (whitespace, punctuation outside `_`)
   - starts with digit
   - reserved word
   - NUL byte (`\0`)
   - path-traversal segment (`..`)
   - embedded newline / carriage return
   - other control chars (U+0000..U+001F, U+007F)
   - non-NFC Unicode that normalizes into a different identifier
   Each case produces a META-IDENT-* diagnostic with the metadata
   span pointing at the offending object. (This bead is the canonical
   home for these path-traversal / NUL / control-char cases per the
   bsf.2 fixture-home index.)
2. Parser-validation gate has a negative test: inject a malformed SQL
   string into the emitter and verify the writer never sees it.
3. Identifier validation uses a typed error: META-IDENT-* codes with
   spans pointing back at the metadata object that caused the failure.

Do NOT:
- Skip the parser-validation gate even for trusted outputs; this is
  a load-bearing safety net.
- Use raw string concatenation to build SQL anywhere in the codebase
  (fitness test guards generator crates from depending on a regex/
  string-templating crate that could enable this).

title: Live PostgreSQL execution validation in CI
description:
Generated DDL must execute against real PostgreSQL versions in CI
(NFR-R4). This bead wires up the matrix and the test harness.

PRD refs: §11.2 (compatibility), §20.1 (CI checks), NFR-R4, GEN-001
acceptance #3.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. CI matrix runs PG 16, 17, 18 (or current N-2..N) using a service
   container.
2. Each run: clean DB -> execute generated schema.sql -> query
   information_schema to verify expected tables/columns/constraints
   exist.
3. Test asserts FKs are enforceable: insert valid + invalid rows and
   verify FK violations raise.
4. Test asserts NOT NULL and UNIQUE constraints are enforced.
5. Test runs against the Phase 1 demo fixture.
6. Expected runtime <2 minutes per PG version on standard CI.

title: Markdown documentation generator — full Phase 2A scope
description:
Promote the Phase 1 PoC docs generator to full Phase 2A scope: all
entities, attribute tables, business keys, relationship lists, source
mapping summaries, governance summaries, open questions, and
documentation quality warnings.

PRD refs: §11.5 (documentation generation), §19.3 (generated project
docs), GEN-004, §22 build sequence #14.

Generated content (PRD §11.5):
- Project overview (generated/docs/README.md or index.md).
- Per-entity page at generated/docs/entities/<entity_name>.md.
- Plain-language summary first.
- Attribute table: name | logical type | semantic type | required |
  unique | constraints | governance labels | description.
- Business key callout.
- Relationship section (FK in / FK out, M2M).
- Source mapping summary (which sources feed this entity, which columns
  map).
- Governance summary (owner, steward, classification, sensitivity, PII
  flags, retention policy) with stakeholder-friendly labels.
- Open questions section (those whose related_objects include this
  entity).
- Assumptions section with confidence labels.
- Documentation quality warnings (missing description, missing required
  attribute description, etc.) — informational, not blocking.

Quality requirements (PRD §11.5):
- Stakeholder-friendly translations of constraints (e.g., "Email
  addresses up to 320 characters" instead of "VARCHAR(320)").
- Distinguish confirmed metadata from assumptions, unresolved
  questions, inferred defaults, and policy-provided defaults.
- "Questions to confirm" section for incomplete metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Demo fixture produces complete docs that render correctly in GitHub
   Markdown viewer (snapshot test against a recorded HTML render).
2. Output is deterministic and uses LF line endings.
3. Generated links between pages are validated (pre-write, the docs
   generator asserts every internal link target exists in the artifact
   set).
4. Manifest records every page with metadata_object_ids and trace
   spans.
5. Stakeholder-friendly language uses a small phrase table that lives
   in know_now_gen_docs (no ad-hoc per-call language).

Do NOT:
- Generate a Mermaid ER diagram here — that is Phase 2B (GEN-005).
- Use JavaScript or HTML beyond <!-- comments -->.

title: Generation manifest writer (Phase 2A — full)
description:
Promote the Phase 1 manifest schema to a fully populated writer that
records every Phase 2A artifact, the resolved policy pack, the lockfile
hash, target database settings, and trace data for explain.

PRD refs: §8.11 (manifest example), GEN-010, AGENTS.md §4.2, §8.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Manifest matches the §8.11 example shape exactly (engine_version,
   metadata_schema_version, generator_contract_version, project_id,
   input_hash, lockfile_hash, target_database, policy,
   template_renderers (empty in Phase 2A), artifacts[], warnings[]).
2. Artifacts are sorted lexicographically by path; trace entries are
   sorted by artifact_span.line_start.
3. Manifest writer is in know_now_writer; generator crates produce
   ArtifactDescriptors that the writer turns into manifest rows.
4. Manifest exclusions verified by test: NO timestamps, hostnames,
   user names, or absolute paths.
5. Manifest survives identical-input determinism check on Linux/macOS/
   Windows (NFR-R1).
6. JSON Schema for the manifest is exported under docs/dev/.

Do NOT:
- Fold volatile run state into the manifest (that lives in
  .knownow/, AGENTS.md §4.2).
- Embed self-hash of the manifest file inside the manifest
  (chicken-and-egg; rely on external integrity if needed).

title: Acceptance Criteria
description: - One

title: Epic — Phase 2B: dbt, quality, diagrams, artifact validation
description:
Phase 2B expands generation coverage once the metadata graph, lockfile,
artifact writer, and diagnostics are stable from Phase 2A.

PRD refs: §6.3 (scope), §22 build sequence #20-#25, §23.3 (exit criteria),
§8.10 (dbt toolchain adapter), §11.3-§11.6 (artifact specs).

Included:
- dbt project generation (dbt_project.yml, sources, staging models, mart
  models, schema tests).
- dbt tests + custom generic tests from constraints/semantic types.
- Provider-neutral quality contract YAML (PRD §11.4).
- Mermaid ER diagram generation (PRD §11.6).
- Optional dbt toolchain adapters for compile validation
  (none / dbt / dbt-core / dbt-fusion / docker — PRD §8.10).
- Generated artifact validation beyond PG DDL and Markdown.
- Deterministic demo fixture data (GEN-015, PRD §11.9).
- Expanded compatibility-fixture suite for dbt and quality outputs.

Exit criteria (PRD §23.3):
- Generated dbt project compiles in CI where validation is configured.
- Generated quality contracts cover all generated quality expectations.
- Mermaid diagrams validate.
- Demo fixture data deterministic when enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures classify generated output changes.
- External validation adapters are capability-detected and optional unless
  explicitly required by project config.

Architecture invariants reinforced here:
- dbt is NEVER a know-now runtime dependency — only a configurable external
  validation target (PRD §8.10, NFR-I7).
- Each new generator crate is independent: no cross-deps between generator
  crates (NFR-M1, NFR-SC2). Adding a new target must not require modifying an
  existing generator.

## Success Criteria

Close this epic only when its child beads prove the described scope, every phase or NFR gate is linked to evidence, and remaining follow-up work is explicitly deferred into separate beads.

title: dbt project generation — sources, staging, marts, schema.yml
description:
The dbt project generator. Output is conventional and readable so that
real data engineers can run, inspect, and customize it (PRD §4.2).
Implementation is split across four child tasks (8vf.1.5 dbt_project.yml,
8vf.1.6 sources.yml, 8vf.1.7 staging models, 8vf.1.8 mart models); this
parent bead is the integration story.

PRD refs: §11.3 (dbt generation), §9.6 (dbt ownership model), GEN-002,
§22 build sequence #20.

Generated layout (PRD §9.6):
  generated/dbt/
    dbt_project.yml         (includes both generated + custom paths)
    sources.yml             (one source block per source system)
    models/
      staging/
        stg_<source>__<table>.sql   (one per source table)
        schema.yml
      marts/
        <entity_name>.sql           (one per entity)
        schema.yml
    tests/
      generic/                      (Phase 2B test bead — 8vf.2)

User-owned dbt artifacts live under custom/dbt/ (PRD §9.6) and are
never written by know-now. The generated dbt_project.yml's `model-paths`
includes both generated/dbt/models and custom/dbt/models so dbt sees
both.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each child task's acceptance criteria pass (8vf.1.5 dbt_project.yml
   emitter, 8vf.1.6 sources.yml emitter, 8vf.1.7 staging models,
   8vf.1.8 mart models).
2. Integration: the four child emitters compose into a single dbt
   project that `dbt parse` accepts under 8vf.4 mode=dbt against
   dbt-core 1.7+; output is byte-identical across two consecutive
   runs on the demo + governed fixtures (NFR-R1).
3. custom/dbt/ untouched after generate (EXT-001 acceptance) —
   verified by an integration test that pre-populates custom/dbt/
   with sentinel files and asserts unchanged after a full generate.
4. Generated dbt project compiles end-to-end via 8vf.4 mode=dbt
   (Phase 2B "dbt validation" bead exercises this).
5. NFR-I2 dbt version pinning: dbt_project.yml carries the project's
   pinned dbt version constraint (verified at the parent integration
   level; subtask 8vf.1.5 owns the emitter).

Do NOT:
- Inline business logic in mart models. Marts are 1:1 with entities and
  expose only the columns + relationships defined in metadata. Custom
  business logic belongs in custom/dbt/.
- Embed timestamps anywhere in generated dbt files.
- Re-author subtask ACs here; this parent owns the integration story
  only.

title: dbt_project.yml emitter — model-paths includes custom/dbt
description:
Emit a dbt_project.yml that wires generated/dbt/models AND
custom/dbt/models together so dbt compiles both as one project.

PRD refs: §9.6 (dbt ownership), §11.3.

Required keys:
- name, version, profile, config-version
- model-paths: ["generated/dbt/models", "../custom/dbt/models"]
  (relative to dbt project root)
- macro-paths: ["../custom/dbt/macros"]
- seed-paths: ["../custom/dbt/seeds"]
- exposure-paths: ["../custom/dbt/exposures"]
- tests: pinned defaults from policy
- vars: derived from project config
- on-run-start/on-run-end: empty unless configured

Acceptance:
1. Compiles cleanly when custom/dbt/ is empty.
2. Compiles cleanly when custom/dbt/ contains a model with the same
   table name as a generated model (the user's wins; warn from
   know-now diff --scan-custom in Phase 3).
3. Output deterministic.

description:
Emit a dbt_project.yml that wires generated/dbt/models AND
custom/dbt/models together so dbt compiles both as one project.

PRD refs: §9.6 (dbt ownership), §11.3.

Required keys:
- name, version, profile, config-version
- model-paths: ["generated/dbt/models", "../custom/dbt/models"]
  (relative to dbt project root)
- macro-paths: ["../custom/dbt/macros"]

- exposure-paths: ["../custom/dbt/exposures"]
- tests: pinned defaults from policy

1. Compiles cleanly when custom/dbt/ is empty.
2. Compiles cleanly when custom/dbt/ contains a model with the same
   table name as a generated model (the user's wins; warn from

3. Output deterministic.

title: dbt source definitions emitter (sources.yml)
description:
Emit sources.yml from metadata.sources[]. One source block per system,
one table per declared source table.

PRD refs: §11.3, §10.6 (source mapping definition).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each source has freshness when sources[].freshness is configured.
2. Loaded_at_field uses sources[].tables[].incremental_key when present.
3. Tables are sorted alphabetically.
4. Quoting set per target_database conventions.

title: Staging model emitter (stg_<source>__<table>)
description:
One staging model per source table. Renames source columns to target
names, applies type casts derived from logical/semantic types, and
declares materialization=view (per dc_standard policy default).

PRD refs: §11.3, §10.6 (source column maps).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Output uses dbt's source() macro, never hard-coded table names.
2. Column casts use Postgres types via the same mapping as PG DDL
   (consistency check: a property test verifies stg model column types
   match the corresponding PG DDL column types).
3. Comments link the staging model to the source table by ID.

title: Mart model emitter (one per entity)
description:
One mart model per entity. Joins or unions staging models that target
the entity (per source mapping target_entity), exposes the canonical
column set defined by the entity's attributes, and materializes as
table by default.

PRD refs: §11.3, §10.4 (entity definition).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. When an entity has exactly one source mapping, the mart selects from
   that one staging model.
2. When an entity has multiple source mappings, the mart unions them
   with explicit source_id discriminator + DEDUPE on business_key
   (warn in docs that this is naive; users may override in custom/).
3. Mart includes ref() to staging models, never raw source().
4. Comments cite metadata object IDs (entity + attribute).

title: dbt tests + provider-neutral quality contract
description:
Two complementary outputs:

1. dbt tests in generated/dbt/models/*/schema.yml + custom generic
   tests in generated/dbt/tests/generic/.
2. Provider-neutral quality contract YAML at
   generated/quality_contracts/<entity>.yml that records EVERY
   generated quality expectation in a stable know-now schema.

PRD refs: §11.4 (quality generation), GEN-003, §22 build sequence #21.

Test sources (PRD §11.4):
- attribute.required = true        -> not_null
- attribute.unique = true          -> unique
- semantic_type=identifier on PK   -> not_null + unique
- semantic_type=email              -> not_null when required + custom
                                      generic test "is_valid_email"
- relationship                     -> relationships test on FK
- attribute.constraints.max_length -> custom generic test
                                      "max_length"
- attribute.semantic_type=country_code/postal_code/status -> accepted
  values where the policy pack provides a list
- attribute.semantic_type=currency_amount -> not negative (when
  attribute.constraints.minimum >= 0)

Provider-neutral quality contract schema (know-now's own schema, NOT
Great Expectations or Soda):
  version: "1.0"
  entity: ent_customer
  checks:
    - id: chk_customer_id_not_null
      target:
        kind: column
        attribute_id: attr_customer_id
      kind: not_null
      severity: error
      origin: { source: dc_standard_v1, rule: POL-REQ-001 }
    - id: chk_customer_email_format
      target: { kind: column, attribute_id: attr_customer_email }
      kind: regex_match
      pattern: "^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$"
      severity: warning

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Constraints produce dbt tests where possible (acceptance #1).
2. Semantic types produce relevant checks (acceptance #2).
3. Quality contracts include all generated checks even when the dbt
   adapter cannot express the check (acceptance #3, #4).
4. Provider-neutral schema is JSON-schema-validated and stable enough
   for downstream tools to consume.
5. Tests in dbt schema.yml use stable test names; custom generic tests
   live in tests/generic/<test_name>.sql with documented signatures.

Do NOT:
- Generate Great Expectations / Soda configuration in Phase 2B; that
  is an optional provider exporter (PRD §11.4) for after dbt quality is
  stable.
- Embed env-specific connection strings.

title: Mermaid ER diagram generation
description:
Generate Mermaid ER diagrams that render in GitHub Markdown and (Phase
3) the dashboard. Diagrams cover all entities, all relationships,
cardinality, self-referencing, and many-to-many handling.

PRD refs: §11.6 (ER diagrams), §13.5 (dashboard graph + table fallback),
GEN-005, §22 build sequence #22.

Output:
- generated/diagrams/er/<scope>.mmd                 standalone Mermaid
- generated/diagrams/er/<scope>.md                  Markdown wrapper
                                                    embedding the .mmd
- Optional per-domain diagrams when the project has >1 domain.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All entities appear (acceptance #1).
2. All relationships appear (acceptance #2).
3. Cardinality is represented (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many,
   self-reference).
4. Mermaid syntax validates (acceptance #4) — wire mermaid-cli or a
   Rust mermaid validator into CI (NFR-R3 snapshot + syntax test).
5. Many-to-many relationships are drawn through an associative entity
   when the metadata declares one; otherwise they appear as a labeled
   M:N edge.
6. Output is deterministic (sorted entities + relationships).

Do NOT:
- Bundle a Mermaid runtime in the binary; rely on consumer rendering
  (GitHub, dashboard).
- Use experimental Mermaid 11+ syntax that older renderers do not
  support without a configured fallback.

title: dbt toolchain adapter — none/dbt/dbt-core/dbt-fusion/docker
description:
Capability-detected adapter for optional dbt validation. dbt is NOT a
runtime dependency of know-now (PRD §8.10, NFR-I7); this bead wires
optional validation through configurable modes.

PRD refs: §8.10 (dbt toolchain adapter), NFR-I7, NFR-R9, §22 build
sequence #23.

Modes:
- none           generate dbt artifacts; do not compile locally.
- dbt            run a configured dbt executable; detect identity via
                 --version and classify (core | fusion | compatible |
                 unknown).
- dbt-core       require detected dbt Core-compatible behavior.
- dbt-fusion     require detected Fusion-compatible behavior + supported
                 adapter capabilities.
- docker         run validation in a configured container image.

Configuration (PRD §8.10):
  dbt:
    validation:
      mode: dbt
      executable: dbt
      required_in_ci: true
      docker_image: ghcr.io/...   (mode=docker only)

Adapter behavior (PRD §8.10):
- Detect executable identity via --version.
- Validation gates are CAPABILITY-based, not name-based.
- Record detected dbt version, adapter availability, supported commands,
  validation limitations.
- doctor reports clear warning/error before generation validation when
  Fusion-compatible validation is configured but the generated target,
  adapter, or command set is unsupported.
- Compatibility matrix (NFR-I1) records dbt Core versions, Fusion
  versions, supported adapters, generated target compatibility,
  validation commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. mode=none works without dbt installed.
2. mode=dbt detects dbt-core 1.7+, classifies as `core`.
3. mode=dbt detects dbt-fusion (when available), classifies as `fusion`.
4. mode=docker runs in container; image must be pinned by digest in
   --locked mode.
5. validation runs `dbt parse` + `dbt compile` against the generated
   project at minimum; `dbt build` is opt-in.
6. Failures surface as diagnostics with file/line where dbt provides
   them, otherwise as command-level errors with stderr captured.

Do NOT:
- Hard-code an executable path. Always go through the configured
  adapter.
- Ship a default mode of `dbt` — default is `none` so know-now works
  for users without dbt.

title: Generated artifact validation expansion (parser + dbt + Mermaid)
description:
Promote the Phase 2A SQL parse-validation gate to a full multi-format
validation step that runs after generation but before promote.

PRD refs: §11.7 (artifact definition of done), §8.5 step 12, GEN-008,
NFR-R5, §22 build sequence #24.

Validators:
- SQL: parse via sqlparser-rs (Phase 2A — extend to all dialect/version
  combinations as targets are added).
- dbt: optional, via dbt toolchain adapter (mode != none).
- Mermaid: syntax check via a Rust mermaid validator (or shell out to
  mermaid-cli when configured; capability-detected like dbt).
- JSON: structural validation against the corresponding schema
  (manifest, quality contract).
- Markdown: link validation (internal links exist; broken links produce
  warnings).
- YAML: re-parse via the know-now YAML subset to verify what we emit
  could also be authored.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All files produced deterministically from the same metadata hash.
- No user-owned files modified.
- Manually edited generated files detected before overwrite.
- SQL parses successfully.
- PostgreSQL DDL executes in CI (NFR-R4).
- dbt project compiles where dbt validation is configured.
- Generated dbt tests are syntactically valid.
- Generated documentation renders without broken Mermaid syntax.
- Manifest records every artifact path + hash.
- Warnings are classified info / warn / error / blocking.
- Failed generation leaves previous generated output intact.
- Stale generated artifacts are detected and reported.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each validator is wired into the generation pipeline as a discrete
   stage with timing recorded in manifest warnings or volatile run log.
2. Failures are reported with generator + artifact + metadata-object
   context (GEN-008 acceptance #3).
3. Validators are capability-detected (NFR-I7).

title: Deterministic demo fixture data (synthetic)
description:
Optional deterministic fixture-data generation for bundled examples
and CI validation. Production projects do NOT generate fixture data
unless explicitly configured.

PRD refs: §11.9, GEN-015.

Output: generated/fixtures/<entity>.csv (or .ndjson) with synthetic
rows that respect logical types, semantic types, required fields, and
simple relationships.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Fixture generation is optional (must be enabled via know-now.yml
   `fixtures: enabled: true` or --target=fixtures CLI flag).
2. Data is deterministic for identical metadata + seed (use a seeded
   PRNG; no system clock).
3. Generated rows respect logical types (e.g., integers fit, decimals
   match precision/scale), semantic types (emails are valid-looking,
   phones are E.164-shaped placeholders, country codes from ISO list),
   required fields (no nulls), and simple relationships (FK values
   exist in parent entity output).
4. Each output file is clearly marked as synthetic via a header line
   or sidecar README in the directory.
5. Production-profile init disables fixture generation by default.

Do NOT:
- Generate plausible PII patterns (names, real addresses, real phone
  numbers); use obviously-synthetic placeholders.
- Use any system entropy source. Determinism is the whole point.

title: Expanded compatibility-fixture suite (dbt + quality + diagrams)
description:
Promote the Phase 2A compatibility-fixture suite to cover the new dbt,
quality, and diagram targets. CI produces a fixture diff summary when
generated outputs change (PRD §20.2).

PRD refs: §20.2 (compatibility fixtures), NFR-R3, §23.3.

Add fixtures (PRD §20.2):
- dbt existing-stack project   pre-existing custom/dbt/ tree to verify
                                no overwrite + correct path wiring
- governed project              policy pack required + governance
                                metadata
- documentation-quality-warning project   missing descriptions
- policy-drift project          (Phase 3 — defer the bead)
- template-pack project         (Phase 3)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each fixture's expected outputs are committed under
   fixtures/snapshots/.
2. fixture diff summary classifies changes as: expected formatting,
   metadata schema, generator behavior, policy default, bug fix,
   breaking (PRD §20.2).
3. CI fails when an unexpected diff appears unless the commit body

title: Phase 2B comprehensive snapshot/integration/property/E2E test suite + verbose logging
description:
Phase 2B comprehensive test suite: snapshot, integration, property-based,
compatibility-fixture, and end-to-end coverage for the dbt project,
provider-neutral quality contract, dbt tests, Mermaid ER diagram,
deterministic demo fixture data, and the dbt toolchain adapter. Mirrors
63g.18 (Phase 2A test suite) for Phase 2B's new generators and adapters,
and integrates the structured logging facade from 42e.10 so every
assertion can read the event stream rather than scraping stdout. Stage
taxonomy is canonical in 42e.10 (docs/dev/logging.md); span names
called out below are the Phase-2B-specific entries within that taxonomy.

PRD refs: §6.3 (Phase 2B scope), §8.10 (dbt toolchain adapter),
§11.3-§11.6 (artifacts), §11.7 (artifact validation), §17.3 (NFR-R3..R5,
R9, R10), §20.1 (CI checks), §20.2 (compatibility fixtures), §23.3
(Phase 2B exit criteria), AGENTS.md §4.2.

Test layers (each layer mandatory; coverage gaps fail this bead):

1) Snapshot tests (insta or equivalent), stable across OS:
   - know_now_gen_dbt — dbt_project.yml, sources.yml, every staging +
     mart model, schema.yml, generic test SQL.
   - know_now_gen_quality — provider-neutral quality contract YAML
     (ALL check kinds: not_null, unique, regex_match, accepted_values,
     min/max, relationships).
   - know_now_gen_docs — Mermaid output (.mmd + .md wrapper) including
     self-referencing + many-to-many fixtures.
   - Manifest writer — Phase 2B-extended manifest with
     template_renderers (still empty), expanded artifact list.
   - Diagnostics — text + JSON for the new generator validation
     errors.

2) Integration tests (real workspace, real artifact writer):
   - End-to-end on demo + governed + dbt-existing-stack + missing-ID +
     rename-heavy fixtures: init -> validate -> check -> generate ->
     assert artifact set + manifest hash + every generator's outputs +
     log-event stream covering every stage.
   - Atomic-failure preservation under generator-mid-list failure
     (NFR-R7) using a deliberate generator-error injection harness.
   - Manual-edit detection on dbt model files (NFR-R11).
   - Stale artifact handling when a source mapping is removed (NFR-R12).
   - --target=dbt|quality|diagrams|fixtures isolated runs each produce
     deterministic subsets.
   - --target=all matches full default behavior.
   - dbt validation modes none/dbt/dbt-core/dbt-fusion/docker each
     covered. mode=none is the default and runs everywhere; mode=dbt is
     a CI-matrix job; mode=docker is a CI nightly job using a pinned
     image digest in --locked. mode=dbt-fusion is best-effort and skipped
     where Fusion is unavailable, with the skip recorded.

3) Property-based tests (proptest):
   - Random valid metadata graphs: dbt staging columns match PG DDL
     columns by logical+semantic type (consistency property; the same
     mapping must hold in both generators — NFR-SC2 hint).
   - Random valid metadata graphs: every constraint produces both a
     dbt schema test AND a corresponding quality contract entry
     (GEN-003 acceptance #3).
   - Mermaid: every relationship in the graph appears as exactly one
     edge in the diagram (no dupes, no drops).
   - Quality contract round-trip: parse the generated YAML back into
     a structured form, assert lossless.

4) Compatibility fixtures (PRD §20.2, §23.3):
   This bead OWNS authoring of the three Phase 2B-additive fixtures
   (was 8vf.7 — now closed and merged here). Fixture-diff classification
   CI gate is owned by bsf.9 AC #5.
   - dbt existing-stack project — pre-existing custom/dbt/ tree to
     verify no overwrite + correct path wiring.
   - governed project — policy pack required + governance metadata.
   - documentation-quality-warning project — missing descriptions.
   - large 100-entity project (carry-over from 2A) — performance proof.
   - rename-heavy + missing-ID projects (carry-over) — exit-readiness.
   For each, fixture diff classification follows §20.2: expected
   formatting / metadata schema / generator behavior / policy default /
   bug fix / breaking.

5) End-to-end shell driver (`cargo xtask e2e phase-2b`):
   Span names below are Phase-2B entries within the canonical taxonomy
   defined by 42e.10 (docs/dev/logging.md).
   - Runs the full pipeline against the demo fixture with --verbose
     --format json; redirects events to events.jsonl per run.
   - Asserts every Phase 2B generator span (gen.dbt, gen.quality,
     gen.docs.mermaid, gen.fixtures) appears with duration_ms within
     budget (NFR-P4/P5).
   - Asserts dbt parse + dbt compile (when mode != none) emit their
     own spans tagged adapter.dbt with detected dbt classification
     (core / fusion / compatible / unknown — NFR-I7).
   - Asserts no log content leaks into generated/ or
     generated/manifest.json (NFR-R13 + fitness #4).
   - On failure, dumps events.jsonl + .knownow/staging/<run_id>/ +
     dbt logs as CI artifacts.

6) Live behavioral validation (NFR-I4):
   - Generated dbt project compiles in CI with mode=dbt against
     dbt-core 1.7+; failures surface with file/line.
   - Generated quality checks tested against valid + invalid fixture
     data (NFR-R5) — fixtures live under fixtures/quality_data/ as
     CSV/NDJSON; invalid fixtures must trigger the matching check.
   - Generated DDL still executes (carry-over from 2A) on PG 16/17/18.

Coverage gates:
- Coverage >=90% overall, >=95% for know_now_gen_dbt + know_now_gen_
  quality + know_now_gen_docs + know_now_toolchain (NFR-R2).
- Mutation-style tests for the dc_standard rules added in Phase 2B
  (e.g., quality severity defaults).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo xtask check + cargo xtask e2e phase-2b green on the reference
   CI machine.
2. Cross-OS CI green (NFR-PO1, NFR-R1).
3. Compatibility fixture diff suite produces a release-notes-ready
   classification per PRD §20.2 (CI gate owned by bsf.9 AC #5).
4. Live dbt compile + DDL execution green for the configured matrix.
5. Coverage gate met.
6. Logging assertions: events.jsonl per fixture is committed under
   tests/e2e/phase_2b/expected_log_shape/ as a JSON-Schema sketch
   (only stage names + field presence — never duration_ms values).

Do NOT:
- Use real timestamps or random IDs in snapshots — SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH
  + seeded PRNG (PRD §8.11).
- Hard-code OS-specific path separators in expected snapshots.
- Skip the dbt mode=none coverage in favor of mode=dbt — mode=none
  must run on every PR (it is the default for users without dbt).
- Mock the dbt adapter when running mode=dbt; use a real dbt binary
  in the CI image, version-pinned for reproducibility.
- Snapshot the structured log durations; only stage names + ordering
  + field presence.

notes:
SUPPLEMENTAL SCOPE (gap-close round 2; explicit cross-cutting redaction
fuzz + audit-flow tests):

Cross-cutting secret-redaction property/fuzz suite (the NFR-S anchor
that 42e.10, hrw.13, hrw.24, and hrw.8 all rely on):

Pattern catalog under tests/security/secret_patterns/:
- High-entropy: 32+ char alphanumeric strings randomly generated
  with seed.
- AWS-style: AKIA + 16 alphanumeric prefix, 40-char secret access
  key.
- JWT-shape: three base64url segments separated by dots.
- PEM block: -----BEGIN ... ----- ... -----END ... -----.
- Generic password shapes: KEY=VALUE, password=… in URL queries.
- SSH private-key prefixes.
- Bearer token shape: Bearer <token>.
- Local environment leakage: $HOME, $USER, hostname, current
  working directory.

Injection paths (assert zero leak in destination for each):
1. CLI command via every Phase 2A + Phase 3 subcommand:
   - flag values
   - positional args
   - stdin
   - environment variables matching the *_TOKEN / *_KEY / *_SECRET
     pattern
2. Server endpoints:
   - request headers (Authorization, Cookie, X-API-Key, etc.)
   - query strings (the launch token route is a controlled exception
     and its value is redacted by hrw.13's route-specific filter)
   - request bodies (POST /api/v1/generate)
3. Audit log entries (hrw.24) — every recorded command path.
4. Support bundle generation (hrw.8) — every included file category.
5. Template-pack rendering (hrw.22) — template inputs.

Destinations to scan (all must be leak-free):
- events.jsonl on stderr
- .knownow/audit.log
- .knownow/runs/<run_id>.json
- generated/manifest.json
- generated/** (text artifacts only — DDL, dbt files, docs)
- support bundle archive
- Server response bodies (read endpoints)

Acceptance:
1. `cargo xtask redaction fuzz` (42e.7) is the runner; this bead owns
   the catalog + test logic.
2. Default seed deterministic (snapshotted under tests/security/);
   nightly CI uses a fresh seed widened to 100k iterations.
3. On any leak: test fails, prints offending pattern + destination
   path + JSON Pointer of the leaked field. CI uploads the captured
   stream + seed for repro.
4. Mutation test: removing a redaction rule from
   know_now_audit::redaction must break this suite (so no rule can
   be silently disabled).
5. Documentation under docs/dev/security.md enumerates every pattern
   + every redacted destination so a contributor adding a new
   destination knows where to plug into the suite.

Audit-flow integration test (anchors NFR-S12):
- Drive every Phase 3 CLI subcommand once with a deterministic
  fixture; assert .knownow/audit.log contains exactly one entry per
  invocation; entries are valid JSON Lines that match the
  audit-log schema; no entry contains any value from the
  pattern catalog.
