- NFR-PO3 generated output uses stable line endings + UTF-8 encoding — 42e.9 ACs #3 (LF hexdump), #5 (no BOM), #6 (non-ASCII byte sequence).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Trace table above is kept current; any new NFR-PO item is added with the owning bead's AC reference.
2. Compatibility-matrix OS/arch row in docs/dev/compatibility.md (bsf.9) reflects the platforms green in 42e.9.

title: NFR-Supply-chain — checksums, attestations, SBOM, install verification
description:
NFR-Supply-chain coverage gate. NFR-SS1..SS7 are implemented by 42e.5
(release binary skeleton); this bead is the trace + the two genuinely
additive items.

PRD refs: §17.8 (NFR-SS1..SS7), §20.3 (release artifacts), AGENTS.md §7.7.

Trace (each line cites the 42e.5 AC that proves the item):
- NFR-SS1 release binaries built in CI from tagged commits — 42e.5 AC #1 + #2.
- NFR-SS2 SHA-256 checksums for every binary/archive — 42e.5 AC #3.
- NFR-SS3 artifact attestations (sigstore / SLSA) where practical — 42e.5 AC #8.
- NFR-SS4 SBOMs (cyclonedx / spdx) where practical — 42e.5 AC #8.
- NFR-SS5 cargo-binstall metadata supports prebuilt binary discovery — 42e.5 AC #4.
- NFR-SS6 source-build fallback `cargo install --locked know-now --version <v>` — 42e.5 AC #5.
- NFR-SS7 release CI verifies every install path produces a working `know-now version` — 42e.5 AC #6.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Trace table above is kept current; any new NFR-SS item is added with the owning bead's AC reference.
2. Release-notes template includes a security-disclosure section (CVE references when applicable). [bsf.8-only — not covered by 42e.5]
3. Default install instructions in README + docs match the documented supported paths (PRD §18.1) — verified by a CI consistency check that diffs README install snippets against the matrix in 42e.5 AC #5+#6. [bsf.8-only]
close_reason: Completed — release-notes template + install consistency CI gate

title: Compatibility matrix maintenance + release notes classification process
description:
Compatibility-matrix process owner: maintains docs/dev/compatibility.md
and drives the per-release classification of generated-output changes
per PRD §20.2. Publication of the changelog + release-notes copy is
owned by qlq.9. NFR-I1 trace lives in bsf.4.

PRD refs: §20.2 (release notes classification), §20.4 (compatibility
matrix), AGENTS.md §7.7 (commit body classification).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. compatibility.md template lives in docs/dev/ and follows the schema
   in PRD §20.4 (engine version, metadata schema, generator contract,
   renderer profiles, PG targets, dbt modes, dashboard browsers,
   Node/pnpm/Vite/TS, OS/arch).
2. xtask release prepare bumps the matrix and offers an interactive
   confirmation per row.
3. Release-notes generator (xtask) groups commits by classification:
   expected formatting, metadata schema, generator behavior, policy
   default, bug fix, breaking. Publication of the resulting notes is
   qlq.9 AC #2.
4. CI gate: when commit subject indicates breaking (Conventional
   Commits "!"), the body MUST include a BREAKING CHANGE: footer
   (AGENTS.md §7.7).
5. CI gate: any unexpected fixture diff (across all phases — 63g.18,
   8vf.9, hrw.27/28/29) fails unless the commit body includes the §20.2
   classification. Absorbs the former 8vf.7 AC #3.

title: Epic — Phase 3: change safety, visibility, admin support
description:
Phase 3 adds project change management, dashboard visibility, safe local
serving, traceability, and administrator support. This is where know-now
becomes useful to David (stakeholder) and Sanne (administrator), not only
to Marco (consultant) and Priya (data engineer).

PRD refs: §6.4 (scope), §22 build sequence #26-#37, §23.4 (exit criteria),
§13 (dashboard + local API), §14 (policy packs), §15.1 (template packs).

Included CLI commands (PRD §12.1 Phase 3):
  diff, diff --impact, diff --scan-custom, issues, doctor, serve,
  explain, support bundle, policy status, policy explain, admin scan,
  admin catalog check, review export, id backfill --apply

Included subsystems:
- Stable object ID diffing + rename detection.
- Incremental generation + content-addressed cache (PRD §8.6).
- Deprecation issue store under .knownow/.
- Migration SQL stubs for additive changes and confirmed renames.
- Local audit log under .knownow/audit.log.
- Approved-version catalog + policy/template drift detection.
- Local Rust axum server bound to 127.0.0.1 with launch-token + session +
  CSRF + strict CORS (PRD §13.2, NFR-S14, S15, S16).
- TypeScript/React dashboard at WCAG 2.2 AA for Phase 3 components.
- Versioned local API under /api/v1 + generated TypeScript client.
- Custom declarative template packs via restricted know-now-minijinja-v1
  renderer profile (PRD §15.1, NFR-S22-S25).
- Custom/project policy packs beyond the built-in default profile.

Exit criteria (PRD §23.4):
- diff/issues workflows handle additive, rename, removal, and ambiguous
  changes.
- Incremental generation matches full generation output (NFR-R10).
- doctor catches common setup and project health problems.
- explain traces artifacts to metadata and policy rules.
- support bundle creates sanitized diagnostic bundles.
- admin scan produces usable multi-project governance JSON.
- Dashboard renders entity list, entity detail, graph, graph table fallback,
  docs, manifest, health, review summary, and traceability views.
- Local server defaults are safe.
- Custom declarative template packs work safely through strict, fuel-limited,
  path-isolated rendering.
- Policy pack validation + drift detection are usable.
- Stakeholder review summary usable in a real review meeting.
- ≥2 real consulting engagements (or realistic pilots) use the tool
  successfully.

Hard rules (AGENTS.md §4.4, §8):
- Local server binds to 127.0.0.1 by default; --host 0.0.0.0 requires
  explicit flag + warning.
- API requests require a local session (launch-token exchange, then
  same-site HTTP-only cookie).
- Write endpoints disabled unless --allow-generate; require request-level
  confirmation token in addition to session.
- Templates: no native function/filter/test/loader registration for custom
  packs; no env reads, process exec, network, DB access, or direct file
  writes.

## Success Criteria

Close this epic only when its child beads prove the described scope, every phase or NFR gate is linked to evidence, and remaining follow-up work is explicitly deferred into separate beads.

title: diff command — stable-ID matching, change classification, --baseline
description:
The diff command is the foundation for change-safety workflows: rename
detection, migration stub generation, deprecation issue creation, and
review summaries.

PRD refs: §12.1 (Phase 3 commands), LIFE-003, LIFE-007, LIFE-009, §22
build sequence #26.

Subcommand surface:
  know-now diff
  know-now diff --format text|json|sarif|quiet
  know-now diff --baseline last-generation     (default — diff against
                                                previous manifest input)
  know-now diff --baseline git:<ref>           (diff metadata at that ref
                                                vs. current working tree)
  know-now diff --baseline manifest:<path>     (diff against an arbitrary
                                                prior manifest)
  know-now diff --migration-safe               (require stable IDs; fail
                                                fast if missing)
  know-now diff --impact                       (impact analysis on
                                                generated artifacts —
                                                separate bead)
  know-now diff --scan-custom                  (scan custom/ for refs —
                                                separate bead)

Change classifications (LIFE-003):
- added
- modified
- removed
- renamed (stable-ID match)
- compatible
- breaking
- destructive
- ambiguous (no stable ID and heuristic match below threshold)

Architecture:
- know_now_diff crate.
- Operates on two ProjectGraphs (left/right), produced from the two
  baselines.
- Stable-ID match first; fall back to deterministic heuristic
  (name + path + container) with a confidence score; flag heuristic
  matches for human confirmation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. diff categorizes added/modified/removed/renamed/compatible/breaking/
   destructive/ambiguous.
2. Output supports text and JSON.
3. Stable IDs used where available.
4. Performance: 100 entities <1 s.
5. JSON schema is versioned.

Do NOT:
- Mutate metadata to "fix" missing IDs from inside diff.
- Auto-confirm a heuristic rename in --migration-safe mode.

title: admin scan + admin catalog check (multi-project governance)
description:
Two administrator-facing commands:

admin scan <path> walks the filesystem for know-now project roots and
aggregates their governance state into one JSON document.

admin catalog check validates an approved-version catalog file
(syntax + semver ranges + cross-references) by calling the validator
from hrw.25. Catalog schema is owned by hrw.25.

PRD refs: §12.1, ADMIN-008, ADMIN-009, §22 build sequence #30.

admin scan output per project (ADMIN-008 acceptance #2):
- project ID
- engine version
- metadata schema version
- lockfile status
- policy pack status
- template pack status
- template renderer profile status
- target versions
- issue counts
- last generation status

## Acceptance Criteria
1. admin scan recursively discovers project roots (look for
   know-now.yml).
2. JSON output suitable for external dashboards.
3. Scan does not modify projects (ADMIN-008 #4).
4. Scan can use an approved-version catalog (ADMIN-008 #5) — calls
   hrw.25 classify_drift for each project; returns drift class per
   project.
5. admin catalog check shells out to the hrw.25 validator and reports
   findings via the JSON output shape; this bead owns CLI ergonomics
   (exit codes, --format, --quiet) only.
6. Drift classifications match PRD §14.6 (sourced from hrw.25).

Do NOT:
- Read past .knownow/ from inspected projects beyond what the manifest
  exposes.
- Phone home from admin scan.
- Re-implement catalog schema validation here — call hrw.25.

title: review export command — stakeholder review pack
description:
Export a read-only review pack (Markdown bundle) suitable for sharing
with stakeholders or committing to a separate governance repo. Local-
only; no cloud upload.

PRD refs: §12.1, §13.5 (review features), DASH-009, §22 build
sequence #34.

Output: docs/exported/review_<date>/ tree containing:
- summary.md (entities, relationships, open questions, warnings,
  assumptions, changed items since baseline)
- entities/<entity>.md per entity
- diagrams/ (Mermaid copies)
- manifest_summary.json (manifest hash, metadata hash, generation
  status)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Review summary lists entities, relationships, open questions,
   warnings, assumptions, changed items.
2. Summary can be exported to Markdown.
3. View understandable without reading YAML or SQL.
4. Review items have explicit status values (draft / needs-confirmation
   / confirmed / rejected / deferred from .knownow/review_state.json).
5. Exports include manifest hash, metadata hash, generation status.
6. Never requires cloud services.

Do NOT:
- Write to docs/ except under docs/exported/ (PRD §9.1, §9.2).
- Embed PII or env-specific paths.

title: id backfill --apply (Phase 3 explicit metadata-write)
description:
The first explicit metadata-writing command. Patches metadata YAML to
add stable IDs where they are missing, with preview, backups, and
targeted patching that preserves comments + formatting.

PRD refs: §10.11 (future metadata-writing requirements), §8.8 (id
backfill --apply), META-009 acceptance #6.

Pre-conditions:
- The user has reviewed id backfill --dry-run output (Phase 2A).
- A working tree is clean OR the user passes --allow-dirty.

Behavior:
- For each missing ID, compute a deterministic suggestion.
- Apply targeted YAML patches that preserve comments, blank lines, and
  surrounding formatting (use a structural YAML editor that respects
  the §10.2 subset; do NOT round-trip via serialize/deserialize).
- Write a backup of every modified file under .knownow/backups/
  <timestamp>/.
- Report the diff in unified-diff format.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. backfill --apply previews changes before writing.
2. Preserves comments + formatting where possible (PRD §10.11
  requirement).
3. Backups recoverable.
4. After apply: validate, check, generate must succeed without further
  user action.
5. Tests cover: BOM-prefixed file, file with leading comments, file
  with mixed indent (2-space vs. 4-space).

Do NOT:
- Apply without --apply (the dry-run is the default).
- Write to metadata/ from any other command (PRD §10.11 — this is the
  one explicit exception in Phase 3).

title: Local Rust axum server — localhost binding + launch token + session
description:
The local server serves the dashboard and the JSON API. Read-only by
default; bound to 127.0.0.1; per-session token via launch-token
exchange.

PRD refs: §13.2 (local server), NFR-S14, NFR-S15, NFR-S16, NFR-S20,
AGENTS.md §4.4, §8 (don't bind to 0.0.0.0 by default), §22 build
sequence #31.

Defaults (PRD §13.2):
- bind to 127.0.0.1
- read-only mode
- per-session access token required for browser/API access
- strict CORS + origin checks
- no generation trigger unless --allow-generate
- no telemetry

Launch flow:
- serve prints a local URL with a one-time launch token:
    http://127.0.0.1:3827/__open?launch_token=<UUID>
- Browser visits __open, server exchanges launch token for a session
  cookie (same-site, HTTP-only) and redirects to /, removing the
  token from history (replaceState equivalent or hard-coded redirect
  through 302).
- Subsequent API requests use the cookie; query-string tokens are
  rejected (NFR-S15, AGENTS.md §8).

Network exposure:
- know-now serve --host 0.0.0.0 prints a warning that this server is
  not intended as an authenticated multi-user deployment.

Write endpoints:
- Disabled unless serve --allow-generate.
- Require CSRF protection (double-submit cookie or per-form token).
- Require an explicit request-level confirmation token (form field) in
  addition to session.
- Require a SECOND --allow-generate-on-network flag when bound to
  anything other than 127.0.0.1 (PRD §13.2).
- Visible warning in dashboard + doctor (DASH-007 + CLI-009).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Default bind = 127.0.0.1; --host changes; --host 0.0.0.0 emits
   warning text.
2. Launch token is single-use; reuse → 400.
3. Session cookie: HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Secure when behind TLS.
4. Query-string tokens rejected after launch.
5. CORS allows only the served dashboard origin (NFR-S15).
6. Strict response headers (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options
   DENY) per NFR-S20.
7. Compile-time + runtime write-endpoint guard verified by hrw.27 F.1
   + F.2 (compile without `allow-generate` feature: route absent;
   with feature but without --allow-generate flag: route returns 404).
   This bead implements the guard; hrw.27 owns the verification.

Do NOT:
- Allow Bearer tokens in query strings for normal API requests
  (AGENTS.md §8 hard don't).
- Default to 0.0.0.0 (AGENTS.md §8 hard don't).
- Skip the request-level confirmation token on writes (PRD §13.2,
  NFR-S16).
notes:
SUPPLEMENTAL SCOPE (gap-close round 2; structured request logging + redaction):

Per-request span fields (all emitted via the 42e.10 facade):
- request_id (UUID v4 generated server-side, OR echoed from X-Request-Id
  if the client sent one — length-validated, ASCII-only, max 64 chars
  to prevent log injection).
- method (GET/POST/etc.).
- route_template (the matched route pattern, e.g. /api/v1/entities/{id};
  NEVER the raw path with the user's id substituted, to prevent
  high-cardinality / PII leakage).
- status (HTTP status code).
- duration_ms (request-start to response-end).
- session_present (bool — never the session value).
- write_mode_active (bool — true only when --allow-generate is set).

Redaction rules for HTTP layer:
- Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-API-Key headers are redacted to
  { set: bool } (length not logged, value never logged).
- Query strings stripped from logs entirely (the launch token might
  appear as a single-use parameter on the __open route — see PRD §13.2;
  redacted via a route-specific log filter).
- Request and response bodies are NEVER logged unless --debug is
  passed AND the body is small (< 4 KiB) AND the route is GET. POST /
  PUT bodies never enter the log stream regardless.
- Bind address is logged once at startup; not echoed in body responses
  or in the dashboard's bootstrap payload.

Structured security events (each is a tracing event with a stable name):
- launch_token_issued, launch_token_used, launch_token_invalid_reuse
- session_created, session_invalidated
- write_endpoint_attempt { allowed: bool }
- cors_reject { origin_redacted: bool, reason }
- csp_violation_report (when CSP report-uri is configured)
- csrf_reject { reason }
- network_binding_warning (emitted once when --host != 127.0.0.1)

Acceptance additions:
8. Integration test: 1000 randomly-generated Authorization /
   Cookie / X-API-Key values driven through a /api/v1/* call;
   captured events show zero leak (mirrors bsf.2 redaction fuzz scoped
   to the HTTP layer).
9. Property test: route_template snapshot stable for every registered
   handler (so adding a route requires updating the snapshot, which is
   a forcing function for log-shape review).
10. X-Request-Id behavior tests:
    - client omits → server generates UUID v4.
    - client sends valid → echoed.
    - client sends invalid (>64 chars / non-ASCII / control chars) →
      server generates a fresh UUID v4 + emits security event
      `request_id_invalid`.
11. \"Logs go to stderr only\" test: capture both stdout + stderr on
    serve invocation; assert events.jsonl content appears only on
    stderr. Audit log (hrw.24) is the persistent equivalent.

title: Versioned local API /api/v1 + read endpoints
description:
The /api/v1 surface that the TypeScript dashboard consumes. Versioned
under /api/v1; future contract changes go to /api/v2 with a documented
deprecation window.

PRD refs: §13.3 (API behavior), §13.4 (API contract rules), DASH-008,
NFR-I8, §22 build sequence #32.

Read endpoints (PRD §13.3):
- GET /api/v1/version           engine, API contract, dashboard asset,
                                compatibility status
- GET /api/v1/project           project summary
- GET /api/v1/domains
- GET /api/v1/modules
- GET /api/v1/entities
- GET /api/v1/entities/{id}     full detail
- GET /api/v1/relationships
- GET /api/v1/graph             graph data for dashboard rendering
- GET /api/v1/documentation     entity docs
- GET /api/v1/diagrams
- GET /api/v1/manifest          deterministic manifest
- GET /api/v1/issues            from .knownow/issues.json
- GET /api/v1/health            doctor output
- GET /api/v1/traceability      manifest trace map
- GET /api/v1/policy            findings, drift status
- GET /api/v1/open-questions
- GET /api/v1/review            review summary

Write endpoints (only with --allow-generate):
- POST /api/v1/generate         trigger generation; requires CSRF +
                                request-level confirmation

Contract rules (PRD §13.4):
- All routes under /api/v1.
- /api/v1/version includes engine, API contract version, dashboard
  asset version, compatibility status.
- Dashboard checks API compatibility on startup.
- Bundled dashboard assets + server API contract tested together in CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. GET endpoints return project, entity, graph, documentation, issue,
   policy, traceability, review, manifest data (DASH-008 #1).
2. API documented (OpenAPI under docs/dev/api/openapi.yaml).
3. Generation endpoint disabled unless --allow-generate (DASH-008 #3).
4. API requests require local session.
5. Write requests require explicit server opt-in + request-level
   confirmation.
6. Dashboard displays binding, token/session, and write-mode security
   state.
7. p95 entity list response <200 ms on 100-entity project (NFR-P9).

Do NOT:
- Skip the API compatibility check on dashboard startup
  (NFR-I8 / NFR-M6).
- Add an unversioned /api/ alias.

title: Generated TypeScript API client (from OpenAPI / shared contract)
description:
The dashboard consumes the API only via a generated TypeScript client
or via shared JSON contracts. No hand-written fetch calls.

PRD refs: §13.4, CLI-008, NFR-I8, §22 build sequence #32.

Pipeline:
- Server publishes OpenAPI spec at docs/dev/api/openapi.yaml.
- Build step generates web/src/api/client/ TypeScript client.
- Dashboard imports the typed client; no `fetch()` outside the client
  module.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Generated client lives under web/src/api/client/ and is tracked in
   git (so users without the generator can still build).
2. Build step regenerates the client when openapi.yaml changes; CI
   fails when the committed client diverges from the spec.
3. Strict TypeScript: every endpoint typed; no `any` (AGENTS.md §6.2).
4. Compatibility check: client at startup hits /api/v1/version and
   warns if engine API version differs from the version the client was
   generated against.
5. Tests: mock server tests for each endpoint round-trip.

Do NOT:
- Hand-author client code that bypasses the generated client.
- Vendor a proprietary client generator without dependency-policy
  review.

title: Dashboard — entity list, entity detail, relationship graph + table fallback
description:
Three core dashboard views that David (stakeholder) and Marco
(consultant) use to navigate the project: list of entities, detail
page per entity, and a relationship graph with an accessible table
fallback.

PRD refs: §13 (dashboard scope), §13.5 (UX principles), DASH-001,
DASH-002, DASH-003, NFR-P8, NFR-P9, §22 build sequence #33.

## Acceptance Criteria
- DASH-001 entity list: name, description, type, tags, domain/module,
  governance labels, attribute count; search + filters; perf budget
  met (NFR-P9: <200ms p95 for 100 entities).
- DASH-002 entity detail: attributes, constraints, semantic types,
  logical types, business keys, source mappings, governance metadata,
  open questions, relationships; non-technical phrasing where
  possible.
- DASH-003 graph + table fallback: pan/zoom; click + hover details;
  accessible table fallback for screen readers and large models
  (PRD §13.5).
- WCAG 2.2 AA target for these components (PRD §13.5).
- Keyboard-navigable.
- FCP <1s on demo project (NFR-P8).

Do NOT:
- Render raw YAML in stakeholder-facing views (PRD §13.5 design
  requirements).
- Bind any view to write endpoints by default; this is the read-only
  default surface.

title: Dashboard — generation status + docs viewer + manifest viewer
description:
Dashboard views that surface engine state: generation status (timestamp
from volatile run state, version info, artifact counts, warnings),
generated documentation viewer (renders Markdown + Mermaid), and
manifest viewer.

PRD refs: §13.1, DASH-004, DASH-005.

## Acceptance Criteria
- DASH-004 generation status: last generation timestamp + engine
  version + metadata version + artifact counts + warnings + errors;
  failed runs link to diagnostics.
- DASH-005 docs viewer: Markdown→HTML render; Mermaid render visually;
  navigation by entity/domain/module.
- Failed generation summary shows the last error code with link to
  diagnostics + explain output.
- Performance: docs viewer renders within 500 ms of route change.

Do NOT:
- Re-render Mermaid diagrams via JavaScript when generated/diagrams/
  already has them; reuse the file.
- Embed external CDN scripts; bundle Mermaid into the dashboard.

title: Dashboard — artifact traceability + explain view
description:
Two complementary dashboard surfaces backed by the explain command's
data + the manifest's trace map.

PRD refs: §13.1, DASH-006, GEN-012.

Behavior:
- From an entity page: link to "what this generates" (list of
  generated artifacts that include this entity_id in their
  metadata_object_ids).
- From a generated artifact view: list metadata objects, policy rules,
  template inputs, validation gates, generator + version.
- Sticky navigation between metadata and artifact views.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Entity pages link to generated artifacts.
2. Artifact pages link to metadata object IDs.
3. Manifest data powers the traceability view.

title: Dashboard — health/admin view (DASH-007)
description:
Health/admin view powered by the doctor command output. Used by Sanne
(administrator).

PRD refs: §13.1, DASH-007, ADMIN-002.

Reported (DASH-007 acceptance #1):
- metadata schema version
- generator contract version
- policy pack
- target versions
- template packs (Phase 3 templates story)
- template renderer profiles
- issue counts
- policy drift
- last generation status
- warnings

Plus security state (DASH-008 #6):
- bind address
- token/session state
- write-mode flag

## Acceptance Criteria
1. View lists all DASH-007 acceptance items.
2. Security warnings visible (DASH-007 #2).
3. Template pack name/version/target/renderer profile/trust level
   visible (ADMIN-002 #1).
4. Unsafe or invalid template packs blocked + visible (ADMIN-002 #2).
5. Unsupported renderer profiles reported (ADMIN-002 #3).

title: diff --impact + diff --scan-custom (impact + custom-reference scan)
description:
Two diff subcommand flags that surface the broader impact of a metadata
change on generated artifacts and on user-owned custom/ files.

PRD refs: LIFE-009, EXT-003, §22 build sequence #26.

--impact:
- For each changed metadata object, list the generated artifacts that
  would change (consult the manifest's metadata_object_ids → artifact
  trace map).

--scan-custom:
- Scan files under custom/ for references to changed metadata names or
  stable IDs. Categorize matches:
  - exact stable-ID reference
  - exact name reference
  - heuristic match (substring with surrounding word boundary)
- Performance target: 100 files <5 s (NFR-P7).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. diff --impact (LIFE-009 #1) reports affected generated artifacts.
2. diff --impact --scan-custom (LIFE-009 #2, EXT-003 #4) scans custom
   references.
3. Findings distinguish exact stable-ID, exact name, and heuristic
   matches (LIFE-009 #3, EXT-003 #5).
4. JSON output suitable for CI annotations.

Do NOT:
- Block on incomplete custom scans; categorize partial findings as
  "scan incomplete" warnings.
- Read files outside custom/ for the scan (e.g., ../).

title: Dashboard — review summary, open-question register, review export (DASH-009)
description:
The stakeholder review surface: review summary, open-question
register, change approval summary, plus client-side hooks to the
server's review export.

PRD refs: §13.5 (review features), DASH-009, §22 build sequence #34.

Behavior:
- Review summary: entities, relationships, open questions, warnings,
  assumptions, changed-since-baseline.
- Open-question register: filterable by status, owner, due date.
- Change approval: mark items draft / needs-confirmation / confirmed /
  rejected / deferred. State persists under .knownow/review_state.json
  (PRD §13.5: stored locally; future explicit metadata-writing
  workflow may push some changes back into metadata).
- "Copy stakeholder-safe link" copies a localhost URL with the route
  but no token (token is per-session; recipient must launch from
  serve).
- Export to Markdown: invokes the review export CLI (S_P3_REVIEW) via
  POST when --allow-generate; otherwise links to the CLI command.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Review summary lists entities, relationships, open questions,
   warnings, assumptions, changed items.
2. Summary exportable to Markdown.
3. Understandable without YAML/SQL.
4. Review items have explicit status values.
5. Exports include manifest hash + metadata hash + generation status.
6. Never requires cloud services.

Do NOT:
- Allow review state edits via API without --allow-generate (writes are
  state-mutating).
- Embed dashboard route URLs that include single-use tokens (per-session
  cookies replace tokens after launch — PRD §13.2).

title: WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility for Phase 3 dashboard components
description:
Phase 3 dashboard components must hit WCAG 2.2 AA. Includes the graph
table fallback for screen readers and large models.

PRD refs: §13.5 (design requirements + WCAG 2.2 AA target), DASH-003.

Scope (PRD §13.5):
- Keyboard-navigable entity list, graph alternatives, documentation
  views.
- Sufficient color contrast (verified by automated linter).
- ARIA labels for non-text controls.
- Focus management on route changes.
- Graph table fallback for relationships (DASH-003 acceptance #4).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. axe-core (or equivalent) audits run in CI; serious + critical
   failures block merge.
2. Manual keyboard-only walkthrough documented in
   web/docs/accessibility.md.
3. Screen-reader spot checks documented (NVDA + VoiceOver).
4. WCAG checklist tracked.

Do NOT:
- Treat a11y as an after-thought to retrofit; build it into each
  view bead from the start.

title: Custom declarative template packs — restricted know-now-minijinja-v1 renderer
description:
Phase 3 supports declarative template packs only. Custom packs are
data-only, strict, fuel-limited, path-isolated, and cannot register
arbitrary functions, execute code, access the network, or write files.

PRD refs: §15.1 (declarative template packs), §15.1.1 (restricted
template renderer architecture), EXT-004, NFR-S6 (templates carry
license review metadata), NFR-S22..S25, AGENTS.md §4.4, §8, §22 build
sequence #35.

Template pack manifest (PRD §15.1):
  name, version, target, renderer (kind=know-now-minijinja, profile=1),
  output_dir, permissions (filesystem: output_only, network: none),
  limits (max_templates 100, max_template_bytes 262144, max_output_files
  100, max_output_bytes 10485760, max_fuel 50000, max_include_depth 8),
  trust (built-in | approved | experimental | untrusted),
  licensing (license SPDX identifier, license_url, optional
              license_review status — required for NFR-S6 compliance;
              packs without licensing fail validation in --locked mode)

Renderer profile defaults (PRD §15.1.1):
- profile = know-now-minijinja-v1
- undefined behavior = strict
- custom functions/filters/tests/loaders disabled for custom packs
- dynamic includes disabled
- static includes allowed only inside pack root
- network access = none
- environment access = none
- filesystem reads = template files inside pack root only
- filesystem writes = none (templates return artifact descriptors)
- output writes = artifact writer only
- render fuel required
- output byte / template byte / include depth / file count limits
  required

Built-in pure filters (PRD §15.1.1): snake_case, kebab_case,
pascal_case, upper_case, lower_case, indent, sort_by, join, default,
json, yaml, markdown_escape, html_escape.

Forbidden filters/helpers: current time, random values, environment
lookup, filesystem lookup, network fetch, process execution, database
access, host-specific path expansion.

Pipeline (PRD §15.1.1):
  TemplatePack manifest
    -> manifest validation
    -> license metadata validation (NFR-S6)
    -> template discovery inside pack root
    -> static template safety validation
    -> renderer profile configuration
    -> MiniJinja compilation
    -> strict, fuel-limited rendering against GeneratorContract
    -> artifact descriptors
    -> artifact writer path-safety validation
    -> atomic promotion

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Manifest required.
2. Output confined to declared subdirectory.
3. No arbitrary code execution.
4. Failures isolated + reported clearly.
5. Versions + hashes recorded in lockfile + manifest.
6. Uses know-now-minijinja-v1 profile.
7. Strict undefined behavior.
8. Enforces fuel/template-size/output-size/include-depth/file-count.
9. Custom packs cannot register native functions/filters/tests/loaders.
10. Dynamic include paths rejected.
11. Static includes cannot escape pack root.
12. Diagnostics include template file/line/column/pack/profile/contract
    version.
13. Renderer output passed to writer (NEVER direct write).
14. Trust level visible in doctor + manifest + dashboard.
15. License metadata required (NFR-S6); SPDX identifier validated
    against the cargo-deny license allowlist; `--locked` mode fails
    when a pack lacks license fields or carries a non-allowlisted
    SPDX identifier. Manifest records license + license_url + hash for
    every pack used.

Architecture invariants (NFR-M5 fitness #12-#16, AGENTS.md §4.4):
- know_now_templates produces artifact descriptors only.
- Template rendering cannot access raw YAML parser types.
- Custom packs cannot register native MiniJinja functions/filters/
  tests/loaders (compile-time API gate, not just docs).
- Unsupported renderer profile changes fail compatibility tests.

Do NOT:
- Add a custom function/filter registration API for *custom* packs
  (AGENTS.md §8 hard don't, NFR-S22).
- Allow dynamic include paths (AGENTS.md §8 hard don't).
- Permit untrusted template packs in --locked CI mode unless
  explicitly allowed by policy (PRD §15.1).
- Accept template packs without license metadata (NFR-S6); generated
  output license posture must be auditable from the manifest alone.

title: Custom/project policy packs + drift workflows
description:
Phase 3 expands beyond the built-in default policy pack to support
custom/project policy packs (still declarative) plus pack discovery,
manifest schema, and version-pinning mechanics. The drift workflow
*surfacing* (policy status / policy explain commands) is owned by
hrw.9; the catalog-driven drift classification is owned by hrw.25.

PRD refs: §6.4, §14 (policy packs), ADMIN-001, ADMIN-005, ADMIN-006,
§22 build sequence #29.

Pack discovery:
- Project-local: policy/ directory under the project root.
- Custom: custom/policy/ (referenced from know-now.yml policy.pack).
- Built-in: bundled with the binary (dc_standard).
- Registry packs: future Phase 4.

Pack manifest:
  name, version, description, severity_profile, rules: [{ id,
  severity, applies_to, expression, rationale, remediation }]

Rule expressions are *declarative* (no code execution). Phase 3 rule
DSL covers:
- attribute presence checks
- naming convention regex (anchored)
- enumeration membership (semantic / logical type allowlists)
- relationship cardinality assertions
- tag/governance presence

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADMIN-001 (pinning): config records pack + version; validation fails
  or warns if pack unavailable; manifest records pack + version + hash.
- ADMIN-005 (drift): pack-discovery layer exposes the
  configured/locked/available triple per pack; the user-facing drift
  status report is owned by hrw.9 (policy status), and the
  approved-vs-installed classification is owned by hrw.25.
- ADMIN-006 (explanation): policy explain (hrw.9) consumes the rule
  rationale + severity + remediation fields populated by this pack
  manifest format; orgs can include custom explanation text in pack
  manifest.
- META-010 #6: unknown policy features fail validation in --locked
  unless allowed by experimental profile.

Do NOT:
- Allow rule expressions to execute arbitrary code (PRD §14.4 — packs
  are data-only).
- Auto-merge multiple packs implicitly; users explicitly compose packs
  via know-now.yml.
- Re-implement policy status / policy explain CLI surfaces here — they
  live in hrw.9.

title: Local audit log under .knownow/audit.log
description:
Append-only audit log of CLI operations. Local only; can be disabled
only with explicit configuration.

PRD refs: §9.1, §17.6 (audit-related), ADMIN-003, NFR-S12.

Recorded per command (ADMIN-003 acceptance #1):
- command line
- timestamp
- project root
- metadata hash
- lockfile hash
- engine version
- result (success / failure with error code)

Format: append-only JSONL at .knownow/audit.log. Rotates at 10 MiB
default; archived to .knownow/audit/<datestamp>.log.

Redaction (NFR-S12): secrets never logged; flags whose values may
contain secrets get value-elided.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. ADMIN-003: log records command, timestamp, project root, metadata
   hash, lockfile hash, engine version, result.
2. Secrets never logged.
3. Local + can be disabled only with explicit configuration
   (audit.enabled: false in know-now.yml).
4. Tests cover rotation + redaction.
notes:
SUPPLEMENTAL SCOPE (gap-close round 2; registry-driven fitness coverage):

Registry-driven fitness coverage (anchors NFR-S12 across the entire
CLI surface, including subcommands added in later phases):
- The CLI subcommand registry hook from 63g.1 exposes a list of every
  registered subcommand at runtime. The fitness test enumerates that
  list and, for each subcommand, drives a deterministic fixture
  invocation and asserts:
  a) Exactly one new line appears in .knownow/audit.log.
  b) That line is valid JSON matching the audit-log schema.
  c) The recorded `command` field matches the subcommand name.
  d) Sensitive values from the bsf.2 pattern catalog never appear in
     the line (see also bsf.2's audit-flow test).
- New subcommands added later are automatically covered: if the
  registry contains a subcommand that the fitness test cannot find a
  fixture invocation for, the test fails. This forces the implementer
  who adds a subcommand to register a fixture and confirms NFR-S12
  coverage.

Disable-path test:
- With `audit.enabled: false` in know-now.yml, drive the same
  fixture set; assert zero new lines in .knownow/audit.log; assert
  the in-memory audit-log handle is closed (no file descriptor
  leak).

Failure-path test:
- Drive a subcommand under conditions that produce a validation
  error (e.g., unknown entity reference); assert exactly one audit
  line with `result: \"failure\"` and the recorded `error_code`.

Rotation test:
- Force-grow the log to 10 MiB by writing structured entries until
  rotation triggers. Assert:
  a) Atomic rename (the active file is briefly absent then present
     again; no partial line at the rotation boundary).
  b) Archive lands at .knownow/audit/<datestamp>.log with the
     correct content.
  c) Active log re-opens; subsequent writes append to the new
     file.
  d) The archived file is not pruned automatically (operator
     decides retention).

Mutation test (forcing function for invariants):
- Remove the audit hook from any one subcommand's invocation path
  and rerun the fitness test. Test MUST fail. CI runs this as a
  scheduled mutation job (avoids blocking PRs but catches regressions
  weekly).

Acceptance additions (extend the original list):
5. Fitness test described above lives under tests/audit/ and runs in
   CI on every push.
6. Rotation test runs on Linux + macOS + Windows (NFR-PO1) — Windows
   atomic-rename semantics differ; see writer PoC (upe.9) for the
   approach.
7. Schema documented at docs/dev/api/audit-log-schema.json (parallel
   to doctor schema in hrw.6); mutations of the schema require an
   ADR + lockfile-schema-version bump (PRD §9.5 spirit).

title: Approved-version catalog (separate governance repo) — schema + check + admin scan integration
description:
Administrators define approved policy/template/generator/target/schema
versions in a declarative catalog. Owns the catalog schema definition,
the syntax+semver validator, and drift classification. The
admin scan / admin catalog check / policy status command surfaces are
owned by hrw.10 + hrw.9 (which delegate validation here).

PRD refs: §14.6 (drift detection), ADMIN-009, §22 build sequence #30.

Catalog example (PRD §ADMIN-009):
  approved:
    engines: { know-now: ["1.0.x"] }
    metadata_schema_versions: ["1.0"]
    generator_contract_versions: ["1.0"]
    policies: { dc_standard: ["1.0.x"] }
    templates: { internal_api_docs: ["1.0.x"] }
    template_renderers: { know-now-minijinja-v1: ["1"] }
    targets:
      postgres: { floor: "16", allowed: ["16","17","18"] }

CLI surfaces (owned by other beads, calling into hrw.25):
- know-now admin catalog check <path>     hrw.10 — calls hrw.25 validator.
- know-now admin scan --catalog <path>    hrw.10 — calls hrw.25 drift classifier.
- know-now policy status                  hrw.9  — surfaces the drift output per project.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Catalog schema is the single source of truth — published under
   docs/dev/catalog-schema.md and referenced by hrw.10 + hrw.9.
2. Validator (`know_now_catalog::validate`) checks syntax + semver
   ranges + cross-references; exposed as a library function consumed
   by hrw.10 AC #5.
3. Drift classifier (`know_now_catalog::classify_drift`) returns one
   of {none, patch, minor, major, unknown, unapproved} per PRD §14.6
   for a given (catalog, project_state) pair; consumed by hrw.10 +
   hrw.9.
4. Catalog files are declarative + designed to live in a separate
   governance repository.
5. Unknown / unapproved packs are clearly reported with the originating
   pack name + version + reason.

Do NOT:
- Embed CLI handlers here (those live in hrw.10 + hrw.9). This bead is
  the data model + validator + classifier library only.

title: Phase 3 exit-criteria audit + AGENTS.md status update + pilot sign-off
description:
Closing bead for Phase 3. Walk PRD §23.4 and update AGENTS.md.

PRD refs: §23.4, AGENTS.md §2.

Checklist:
