title: Parser fixture suite — 13 fixtures with text+JSON snapshot tests
description:
Author the parser fixture suite that proves the YAML subset enforcement.
Each invalid fixture snapshots both text and JSON diagnostics so changes to
diagnostic output are visible in code review.

PRD refs: §10.2 (parser diagnostic fixtures), §20.1 (parser fixture tests),
NFR-R14.

Required fixtures (each one file under fixtures/parser/):
- valid_minimal.yml
- valid_multifile_project.yml
- duplicate_key.yml         -> expect META-PAR-DUP error
- anchor.yml                -> expect META-PAR-ANCHOR error
- alias.yml                 -> expect META-PAR-ALIAS error
- merge_key.yml             -> expect META-PAR-MERGE error
- custom_tag.yml            -> expect META-PAR-TAG error
- include_directive.yml     -> expect META-PAR-INCLUDE error
- multi_document.yml        -> expect META-PAR-MULTIDOC error
- deep_nesting.yml          -> expect META-PAR-NESTING error
- large_file.yml            -> expect META-PAR-SIZE error (use a budget low
  enough to keep the fixture small)
- bad_scalar_type.yml       -> expect META-PAR-SCALAR error
- unknown_field.yml         -> expect META-PAR-UNKNOWN error

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each invalid fixture has paired snapshot files: text rendering and
   JSON rendering of the diagnostic.
2. Snapshot tests run via insta (or equivalent) and fail loudly on diff.
3. Error codes are stable strings (no random suffixes); diagnostics tests
   verify the code is present.
4. Fixture file headers comment what they exercise so future contributors
   do not accidentally fix a deliberately invalid fixture.

title: Parser budgets — file size, nesting, expansion guards
description:
Implement and unit-test the configurable parser resource budgets that
prevent denial-of-service through pathological YAML inputs.

PRD refs: §10.2 (parser file-size, nesting, resource-exhaustion limits),
§23.1 (exit criteria), NFR-S13, NFR-S21.

Budgets (defaults; configurable via know-now.yml [parser] table):
- max_file_bytes         (default: 4 MiB)
- max_nesting_depth      (default: 32)
- max_total_nodes        (default: 1_000_000)
- max_anchor_count       (default: 0 — anchors banned, but the budget
  keeps the rejection cheap)
- max_alias_count        (default: 0 — same)
- max_keys_per_mapping   (default: 10_000) catches flatten-explosion

## Acceptance Criteria
1. ParserBudgets struct lives in know_now_metadata::parser::budgets.
2. Each budget has a unit test that triggers it with a small fixture
   (do NOT commit literal multi-MB fixtures — generate at test time).
3. Budget violation produces a META-PAR-SIZE / META-PAR-NESTING error
   with file, position, and current vs. configured limit.
4. Budgets are documented in the user-facing YAML reference so users can
   raise them when needed.

Do NOT:
- Make budgets unbounded by default. Even internal projects should hit
  finite limits to flush out programmer mistakes.

title: marked-yaml fallback assessment + ADR if switching
description:
Run the fallback evaluation gate from PRD §10.2: if the serde-saphyr spike
cannot enforce the know-now subset cleanly, switch to marked-yaml and
record the decision.

PRD refs: §10.2 (fallback rule), §24 (decisions table — Rust YAML parser),
AGENTS.md §7.1 (ADR process), §9.4 (changing versioned compatibility
surfaces).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Spike produces a written assessment (in the bead's mcp-agent-mail
   thread + an ADR draft) covering: subset enforcement quality,
   diagnostic quality, source-span fidelity, parser budget support,
   maintenance posture, dependency depth, and audited license.
2. If serde-saphyr is judged sufficient: ADR records the choice as
   confirmed and links the assessment.
3. If serde-saphyr is judged insufficient: switch to marked-yaml in
   know_now_metadata, update the PRD §24 decisions row, write a
   superseding ADR, and re-run the parser fixture suite.
4. Either outcome must keep the parser dependency isolated to
   know_now_metadata (NFR-M8).

Do NOT:
- Reach for saphyr-parser directly here. That is a Phase-2+ fallback if
  both serde-saphyr and marked-yaml fail (PRD §10.2).
- Allow the project to ship without a decision recorded in the
  decisions table.

title: Source span model — file/line/col/YAML-path index
description:
Define the source-span data model that diagnostics, traceability, manifest
trace entries, and the explain command all rely on.

PRD refs: §10.2 (recommended internal model — SourceSpanIndex), §12.4
(error diagnostics), §16.1 META-003.

Required types (in know_now_metadata::span):
- SourceId(u32) — interned per project parse.
- SourceSpan { source_id, byte_start, byte_end, line_start, line_end,
  col_start, col_end }.
- YamlPath — slash-or-bracket form; e.g., entities[3].attributes[2].name.
- SourceSpanIndex — maps {object_id | YAML path | (source_id, field name)}
  to SourceSpan.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Spans support stable serialization to JSON (used by manifest
   traceability and JSON diagnostics).
2. SourceSpanIndex lookup is O(log n) or O(1) for the common access
   patterns: by metadata object ID, by YAML path.
3. Spans roundtrip through Serde without loss.
4. Tests cover BOM handling, CRLF/LF normalization, and Unicode
   characters that change column counts.

Do NOT:
- Leak parser library types into the public span API. Spans are a
  know-now contract (used by every downstream layer); they must not
  carry serde-saphyr or marked-yaml types.

title: Diagnostics model — codes, severity, text/JSON renderers
description:
The diagnostics model is consumed by parser, validator, policy engine,
generator validation, and CLI/server output. SARIF support is deferred to
Phase 2A; Phase 1 ships text + JSON.

PRD refs: §8.1 (diagnostics layer), §12.4 (error diagnostics), §22 build
sequence #3, META-003.

Diagnostic shape (PRD §12.4):
- severity (info / warning / error / blocking)
- error code (stable string, e.g., META-REL-001)
- human-readable message
- file path
- line / column
- YAML path
- related metadata object ID where available
- suggested fix where deterministic
- JSON representation for CI

Example text rendering target (PRD §12.4 example — match this format
exactly so user-facing docs need not change later):

  error[META-REL-001]: relationship references unknown entity `customers`
    --> metadata/relationships/orders.yml:12:18
     |
  12 |     to_entity: customers
     |                ^^^^^^^^^ unknown entity
     |
  help: did you mean `customer`?

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Diagnostic struct + Severity enum live in know_now_diagnostics.
2. TextRenderer produces the example above byte-identical when given the
   same diagnostic.
3. JsonRenderer emits a stable schema (versioned in
   know_now_diagnostics::JSON_SCHEMA_VERSION) that downstream tools can
   pin against.
4. Renderer trait has no generator-specific types (NFR-M5 fitness test
   #7 guards this).
5. No diagnostic carries a chrono::DateTime, machine path, or
   hostname — diagnostics are deterministic per input (NFR-R1).

Do NOT:
- Introduce SARIF in this bead — that is Phase 2A (CLI-005).
- Use anyhow::Error as a diagnostic carrier; use the typed model.
- Allow generator crates to depend on this crate for anything beyond the
  Diagnostic / Severity types.

title: Authoring metadata structs + typed deserialization
description:
Define the typed Rust structs that the parser deserializes into. These are
the *authoring-schema* layer (PRD §8.3 contract layer 1), distinct from the
normalized graph (layer 2) and the generator contract (layer 3).

PRD refs: §8.3 (metadata contract layers), §10 (metadata model), META-001,
META-002, META-007, META-011, META-012, META-013.

Required types (in know_now_metadata::authoring):
- AuthoringMetadata { version, project, target_database, policy, domains,
  modules, entities, relationships, sources, rules, governance,
  open_questions, assumptions } — top-level document shape.
- Project, TargetDatabase, PolicyRef, Domain, Module.
- Entity { id, name, display_name, domain, module, owner, steward,
  classification, retention_policy, description, type, tags,
  business_key, attributes }.
- Attribute { id, name, logical_type, semantic_type, sensitivity, pii,
  required, unique, constraints, description }.
- Relationship, SourceSystem, SourceTable, SourceColumnMap, QualityRule,
  GovernanceMeta, OpenQuestion, Assumption.
- Enums for LogicalType (PRD §10.7) and SemanticType (PRD §10.8) with
  unknown variants producing typed errors, not silent passthrough.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Every type derives Debug + Clone + Serialize + Deserialize.
2. Custom Serde validators reject unknown fields by default
   (`#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`) so typos surface as
   META-PAR-UNKNOWN errors with span-aware diagnostics.
3. Each enum value matches the PRD spelling exactly (snake_case
   identifiers, no synonyms).
4. Cross-file references (e.g., entity.domain → Domain.id) are not
   resolved here — only typed. Resolution is the graph builder's job.
5. ParsedMetadataDocument wraps AuthoringMetadata + SourceSpanIndex
   (PRD §10.2 example).

Do NOT:
- Resolve cross-file references in this layer.
- Apply policy defaults here. Defaults are an explicit resolution step
  later in the pipeline (PRD §14.4).
- Mutate authoring documents after parse; keep them immutable.

title: Canonical ProjectGraph + semantic validation (≥10 errors)
description:
Build the validated canonical ProjectGraph (contract layer 2) from
AuthoringMetadata. This is the internal source of truth for the engine.
Must catch ≥10 meaningful semantic errors per PRD §23.1 exit criteria.

PRD refs: §8.7 (canonical project graph), §23.1, META-004, META-009,
META-011, §22 build sequence #6.

ProjectGraph contents (PRD §8.7):
- project metadata, domains, modules, entities, attributes, relationships,
  source systems + mappings, business keys, semantic types, logical types,
  quality rules, policy annotations, documentation metadata, governance
  metadata, open questions, assumptions, stable object IDs, source file
  spans, dependency graph, source-to-graph trace map, graph-to-contract
  trace map.

Required validation errors (≥10; codes are illustrative):
1. META-REL-001 unknown entity reference in relationship.
2. META-REL-002 unknown attribute reference in relationship.
3. META-ENT-001 duplicate entity name within a domain.
4. META-ID-001  duplicate stable object ID anywhere in project.
5. META-ENT-002 invalid business_key (not a subset of entity attributes).
6. META-SRC-001 incomplete source mapping (missing target_entity or
   columns).
7. META-DOM-001 unknown domain reference on entity/module.
8. META-MOD-001 unknown module reference on entity.
9. META-ATTR-001 unknown semantic type.
10. META-ATTR-002 unknown logical type.
11. META-Q-001 open question references unknown object.
12. META-ASM-001 assumption references unknown object.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each error has a snapshot test fixture under fixtures/validation/.
2. Each error includes file, line, column, YAML path, error code, and
   the IDs/names of the offending objects (META-003).
3. ProjectGraph is immutable after construction (defensive against the
   "policy mutates graph" failure mode — PRD §14.4).
4. Build cost on the demo fixture <250 ms (informs NFR-P3 budget).
5. Public API exposes traversal helpers (entities by domain, attributes
   by entity, relationships touching an entity) but no &mut access to
   nodes.

Do NOT:
- Reach into parser types from this layer.
- Mutate graph nodes once built; defaults must flow through a separate
  resolution step.
- Allow generators to depend on this crate directly — they consume the
  *projected* generator contract (NFR-M5 fitness test #3).

title: Versioned generator contract draft + capability registry
description:
Define the third metadata contract layer (PRD §8.3): the versioned
GeneratorContract that built-in generators, declarative templates, and
future external generators all consume. Plus the capability registry that
records what each generator can produce.

PRD refs: §8.3 (contract layers), §8.4 (capability registry), §22 build
sequence #6, §23.1 (exit criteria — generator never reads YAML).

GeneratorContract design (PRD §8.3, §11.1):
- Versioned with semver-compatible compatibility tags: contract_version
  (e.g., "1.0").
- Carries the projected view of ProjectGraph that generators need:
  entities + attributes (typed), relationships, source mappings,
  resolved semantic types, resolved logical types, business keys,
  resolved policy defaults with traceability, target_database settings,
  open questions + assumptions for docs.
- Carries graph-to-contract trace map so manifests can record which
  metadata objects produced each artifact (PRD §8.7, §8.11
  metadata_object_ids).
- No raw YAML nodes. No serde-saphyr types. No internal Rust-only graph
  types from know_now_metadata (NFR-M5 fitness #3).

Capability registry (PRD §8.4):
- Each built-in generator declares: generator name + version, accepted
  contract versions, artifact kinds produced, supported target dialects
  + versions, supported logical/semantic type mappings, validation gates
  supported, known unsupported constructs, experimental features.
- Registry is queryable: powers know-now version --capabilities,
  doctor, generation planning, compatibility-matrix generation,
  dashboard health views.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. GeneratorContract lives in know_now_contract crate (no parser deps).
2. CONTRACT_SCHEMA_VERSION constant + JSON Schema export
   (used by Phase 2A schema command).
3. Contract is fully Send + Sync + Clone so independent generators can
   run concurrently (NFR-P13).
4. Capability registry is in know_now_codegen; generators register at
   compile time via a trait + inventory pattern.
5. Fitness test verifies no know_now_gen_* depends on know_now_metadata
   directly (only on know_now_contract / know_now_codegen / know_now_ir).

Do NOT:
- Make the contract a thin re-export of ProjectGraph. The projection step
  is a real piece of work — it materializes resolved defaults and
  trace maps.
- Add unversioned fields. Every contract change requires a version bump
  and a migration note (AGENTS.md §9.4).

title: Deterministic artifact manifest schema (generated/manifest.json)
description:
Define the deterministic artifact manifest written after every successful
generation. The manifest is the cornerstone of explainability,
reproducibility, manual-edit detection, and trace.

PRD refs: §8.11 (deterministic manifest + volatile run logs), GEN-010,
§22 build sequence #5, AGENTS.md §4.2, §8 (no timestamps in manifest).

Manifest example (PRD §8.11) — match exactly:
{
  "engine_version": "...",
  "metadata_schema_version": "...",
  "generator_contract_version": "...",
  "project_id": "...",
  "input_hash": "sha256:...",
  "lockfile_hash": "sha256:...",
  "target_database": {"kind":"postgres","version":"18","compatibility_floor":"16"},
  "policy": {"pack":"...","version":"...","hash":"sha256:..."},
  "template_renderers": [...],
  "artifacts": [
    {"path":"...","kind":"...","artifact_id":"...",
     "generator":"...","generator_version":"...",
     "hash":"sha256:...","metadata_object_ids":[...],
     "trace":[{"artifact_span":{...},"metadata_object_ids":[...],
               "policy_rule_ids":[...]}]
    }
  ],
  "warnings": []
}

Hard rules (AGENTS.md §4.2, §8):
- generated/manifest.json is byte-identical for identical inputs (NFR-R1,
  GEN-007).
- NEVER includes timestamps, machine-local paths, usernames, or
  environment-specific data (PRD §8.11).
- Artifacts list is sorted deterministically (lexicographic by path).
- Hashes use sha256 prefixed with "sha256:" so future hash agility is
  possible.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. ManifestV1 type lives in know_now_writer (or a sibling crate) with a
   stable Serde representation.
2. Manifest schema is exported as JSON Schema and committed under
   docs/dev/ for downstream tools.
3. Two consecutive runs against the demo fixture produce byte-identical
   manifests.
4. Deletion test: removing all timestamps from machine clock + faking
   hostnames does not change the manifest (i.e., they were never
   used).
5. Manifest survives pretty-printing roundtrip (canonical pretty form is
   chosen and tests assert it).

Do NOT:
- Embed full-file content hashes in generated files (AGENTS.md §8 — this
  bead's twin: the manifest holds the hash, the file does not).
- Conflate this with .knownow/last_generation.json which records volatile
  run state (separate bead).

title: Volatile run log under .knownow/ (last_generation.json + runs/)
description:
Implement the volatile run-log files under .knownow/ that record machine-
local execution metadata. Strictly separated from the deterministic
manifest (AGENTS.md §4.2, PRD §8.11).

PRD refs: §8.11 (volatile run logs), §9.1 (project layout), NFR-R13.

Volatile run record example (PRD §8.11):
{
  "run_id": "run_20260502_120000_abc123",
  "started_at": "2026-05-02T12:00:00Z",
  "finished_at": "2026-05-02T12:00:04Z",
  "command": "generate --locked",
  "result": "success",
  "manifest_hash": "sha256:...",
  "duration_ms": 4210
}

Layout:
- .knownow/last_generation.json   — most recent run summary.
- .knownow/runs/<run_id>.json     — per-run history (rolling, capped).
- .knownow/audit.log              — append-only audit (Phase 3 feature
  scaffolded later).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Volatile records may carry timestamps + duration_ms + run_id +
   command line + result.
2. Tests explicitly assert that volatile records do NOT appear inside
   generated/ or generated/manifest.json (NFR-R13).
3. Rolling cap on .knownow/runs/ (default last 50 runs); cleanup is
   safe across crashes (delete-on-success only).
4. .knownow/locks/ contains process-scoped lock files for write
   commands (PRD §9.3).

Do NOT:
- Use the volatile run id in any manifest field.
- Treat .knownow/ as content-addressed; it is mutable per-machine state.
- Promise a stable JSON schema for run records — they may evolve freely.

title: Artifact writer PoC — staging, atomic promote, path safety, locks
description:
Build the proof-of-concept artifact writer that every generator (built-in
and template-pack) writes through. Path safety + atomic staging +
project-scoped locking are foundational to the entire ownership model.

PRD refs: §9.3 (artifact writer rules), §22 build sequence #4, §23.1
(exit: atomic write preserves prior output, path safety rejects writes
outside generated roots), GEN-011, NFR-S17, NFR-R7, AGENTS.md §4.1, §4.3,
§8.

Writer responsibilities (PRD §9.3):
- All generated writes go through know_now_writer.
- Output paths must be relative, normalized, and inside an approved
  generated root.
- Reject absolute paths.
- Reject path traversal (..) and symlink escape.
- Symlinks inside generated output are not followed during writes by
  default.
- Writes acquire a project-scoped lock under .knownow/locks/ for
  generated/ + mutable .knownow/ state.
- Stale generated artifacts are reported.
- --prune stale deletes only files recorded in the previous deterministic
  manifest (NEVER untracked files).
- Previously generated files deleted by the user are reported as missing
  prior artifacts and may be recreated.
- Failed generation preserves the previous generated artifact set.
- Writer behavior tested on Linux, macOS, Windows.

Atomic promotion algorithm (PoC):
1. Compute generation plan + artifact descriptors from generators.
2. Write to staging directory (.knownow/staging/<run_id>/).
3. Validate every artifact (parser/syntax check by kind).
4. Run manual-edit detection vs. previous manifest (Phase 2A — for the
   PoC, just refuse promotion when previous manifest is missing and the
   target file already exists with non-empty content).
5. Atomically replace generated/ with the staging set (rename-based
   promote where possible; fall back to two-phase rename on Windows).
6. Write the new manifest + volatile run log.
7. Clean up staging.
8. On any failure between steps 2-6: discard staging, leave generated/
   intact.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. PoC writer produces deterministic generated/ for the demo fixture.
2. Tests cover: traversal rejection, absolute-path rejection,
   symlink-escape rejection (with a fixture that creates and removes a
   symlink), atomic-failure preservation (kill mid-write), Windows path
   handling.
3. Project-scoped lock prevents concurrent generate runs (file lock
   under .knownow/locks/, OS-portable advisory lock).
4. Deleting the previous generated manifest disables manual-edit
   detection AND emits a clear warning.

Do NOT:
- Allow any generator crate to depend on know_now_writer
  (NFR-M5 fitness #6: writer does not depend on generator crates).
- Embed file-content hashes inside generated files (AGENTS.md §8).
- Use platform-specific path separators in any artifact descriptor.

title: Path safety — traversal, absolute, symlink-escape rejection
description:
The path-safety layer. Every artifact-descriptor path is checked here
before staging; failures abort the whole generation.

PRD refs: §9.3, NFR-S17, NFR-PO2.

Required rejections (one error code per category):
- WRITER-PATH-ABS    absolute path
- WRITER-PATH-TRAVERSE   contains .. components after normalization
- WRITER-PATH-ROOT   not under an approved generated root
- WRITER-PATH-SYMLINK   target traverses a symlink that escapes the root
- WRITER-PATH-WINRES   reserved Windows device names (CON, PRN, AUX, NUL,
  COM1-9, LPT1-9) when on Windows
- WRITER-PATH-NULL   embedded NUL bytes
- WRITER-PATH-NORMAL   path requires platform-specific separators

Acceptance:
1. Each rejection has a unit test fixture and a stable error code.
2. Symlink-escape test creates a real symlink under tempdir, points it
   outside, expects rejection, removes it. Skip on Windows where
   symlinks need admin (use junction or skip per platform).
3. Path normalization is canonicalized to forward slashes for the
   in-memory representation; OS-specific conversion happens only at
   the actual write call site.
4. Cross-platform CI runs this test suite on Linux, macOS, Windows.

title: Atomic staging + rename promote (cross-platform)
description:
The promote step that replaces generated/ with the staging set. On Linux
+ macOS rename(2) is atomic for files in the same filesystem; on Windows
the rename semantics differ for directories — a two-phase swap is
required.

PRD refs: §9.3 (atomic generation), GEN-011, NFR-R7, §22 build sequence #4.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Staging dir lives under .knownow/staging/<run_id>/.
2. After validation passes, the writer either: (a) renames staging dir
   over generated/ (POSIX) or (b) performs a generated -> old + staging
   -> generated + remove-old swap (Windows). Both paths leave generated/
   intact at every observable step.
3. Mid-promote crash test (use a kill signal in tests) verifies that
   either old or new generated/ is intact, never a half-written set.
4. After promote, .knownow/staging/<run_id>/ is removed; failures leave
   it for inspection.
5. Same-filesystem requirement is documented and enforced (refuse to
   promote across filesystems with a clear error).

title: Project-scoped lock under .knownow/locks/
description:
A writer-scoped advisory lock that prevents concurrent generate runs
from corrupting the staging directory or the manifest.

PRD refs: §9.3 (project-scoped lock under .knownow/locks/), §22 build
sequence #4.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Lock acquired on entry to every command that mutates generated/ or
   .knownow/ (generate, lock update, id backfill --apply, review export
   when stateful, support bundle when writing).
2. Read-only commands (validate, check, schema, version, doctor without
   network) do NOT take the write lock.
3. Lock is OS-portable: fcntl on POSIX, LockFileEx on Windows (consider
   the fs2 or fd-lock crate; verify maintenance posture under
   cargo-deny).
4. Lock acquisition timeout is configurable via know-now.yml; default
   30 s.
5. Lock-held diagnostic includes the holding PID + command line +
   acquired_at so users can diagnose stuck locks.

title: Failure preserves prior output (atomic rollback)
description:
Test-driven story that verifies "failed generation leaves previous output
intact" — the property every other reliability claim hinges on.

PRD refs: §9.3, §11.7, NFR-R7, GEN-008.

Failure injection points to test:
- Generator returns an error mid-list (verify staging is discarded).
- Artifact validation fails (parser rejection of generated SQL — verify
  staging is discarded).
- Manual-edit detection blocks promotion (verify previous output is
  intact).
- Disk full during stage write (use a small loopback FS or a quota
  fixture).
- Process kill between stage and promote (use a separate test binary).
- Lock acquisition timeout (verify generated/ is unchanged).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Each failure mode has a named test that asserts byte-identical
   generated/ before and after the failure.
2. Volatile run log records the failure with the failing stage, error
   code, and a pointer to the staging dir for post-mortem.
3. Documented behavior: "When generate fails, generated/ is unchanged
   and .knownow/staging/<run_id>/ is preserved for inspection."
